121887.explanation: The opinion of residents is important. In Peel and Maas, we have extensive experience with participation and self-management. A referendum is not always the right tool. Local Peel and Maas believes that residents should be involved in processes and able to initiate their own initiatives.

121890.explanation: The rural area of Peel and Maas is changing; existing entrepreneurs should receive support to continue their business or to transform it. A shop on the homestead and/or a campsite are some solutions that can count on support from Local Peel and Maas.

121893.explanation: Local Peel and Maas wants to implement the dispersion law. This is a legal task for all municipalities. We take responsibility for the legal part and believe that other municipalities should do the same.

121898.explanation: Local Peel and Maas is not a direct supporter of a fireworks show. If there is a demand for it, we believe that a fireworks show should be organized together with the residents.

121899.explanation: Local Peel and Maas favors placing costs as much as possible where they belong. Making bulky waste collection free means that the collection costs at home are distributed across all residents.

121901.explanation: Accessibility of business buildings is primarily the responsibility of the building owner/entrepreneur. If the entrepreneur has no options within set frameworks, Local Peel and Maas believes support should be possible.

121903.explanation: Local Peel and Maas supports continuing the current policy. People receiving social assistance benefits are supported under the right conditions in paying membership fees for clubs.

121905.explanation: Local Peel and Maas favors good music education at primary school that is accessible to everyone. It also forms a good basis for cultural development among youth.

121906.explanation: As soon as an association has financial worries, Local Peel and Maas wants to help. That does not always have to be through a financial contribution.

121910.explanation: Local Peel and Maas does not want standard camera surveillance. However, we believe that temporary camera surveillance can be a solution to prevent incidents.

121913.explanation: Local Peel and Maas believes that the municipality has an important responsibility towards its residents.

121915.explanation: Holiday homes are intended (and built) for recreational use. Using these homes as a permanent residence is not a good long-term solution.

121916.explanation: Housing is a very large challenge. Local Peel and Maas supports substantially adding homes. This cannot be done solely within current village boundaries.

121917.explanation: Local Peel and Maas is enthusiastic about the municipality’s HAL9 concept. We do not want to apply coercion but prefer tailored solutions. In consultation with the resident, we look at what suits best. Local Peel and Maas believes everyone should be able to participate valuably.

121918.explanation: Local Peel and Maas believes the most vulnerable group of residents deserves protection.

121919.explanation: Local Peel and Maas believes the most vulnerable group of residents deserves protection. The income limit currently used is sufficient. An important condition for Local Peel and Maas is that tailored solutions are always possible.

121922.explanation: Traffic safety is an important priority for Local Peel and Maas. In residential areas, the maximum speed is 30 km/h. Changing the speed limit is not just replacing a sign; roads must also be adapted. Changing road user behavior is also an important focus.

121923.explanation: Local Peel and Maas basically believes everyone should be able to choose from whom they receive care. Quality of care and availability are important conditions.

121925.explanation: Local Peel and Maas does not want to increase property tax, but believes annual indexation is necessary to counter rising costs in the future.

121927.explanation: Local Peel and Maas believes the current rate does not need to be lowered.

121928.explanation: Local Peel and Maas wants to allow expansions provided they add value to the municipality, such as local SMEs. Large block-style buildings are no reason to expand the business park.

121930.explanation: These are national agreements which the municipality does not control.

121932.explanation: Local Peel and Maas primarily chooses for solar panels on roofs of houses and buildings.

121934.explanation: Local Peel and Maas considers swimming lessons important. However, it is not solely the municipality's responsibility to organize this. Where necessary, we want to provide financial support to realize it.

121935.explanation: Local Peel and Maas is not in favor of a coffeeshop.

121936.explanation: Of all new construction projects, 30% is social rental housing. This has been agreed to ensure sufficient social rental homes. Local Peel and Maas does not want to deviate from this.

121941.explanation: Expansion of intensive livestock farming is only possible within existing frameworks. New establishment of intensive livestock farming is not allowed.

121942.explanation: Residents must be able to follow what is happening in their own village and in the municipality as a whole. This can be via a house-to-house paper, online, or a mix. Local Peel and Maas believes the municipality has a responsibility here, working together with initiatives from the villages.

121887.explanation: Decision-making on important topics requires a careful process in which the interests of all parties involved are well weighed. Politics — the municipal council as an elected representative body — is expected to make a well-considered decision.

121890.explanation: It is questionable whether this always fits in the rural area. In some areas, we want to give the agrocluster plenty of space; for the other areas, this can be a good contribution.

121893.explanation: If people must flee war and violence, we must shelter them. We have to deal with the situation as it is. The municipality of Peel and Maas must also take responsibility and contribute to the reception of asylum seekers. We support national policy to limit the inflow.

121898.explanation: A fireworks show is not a task of the municipality. If residents want to organize it, the municipality can support by granting a permit.

121899.explanation: Free collection of bulky waste at home leads to costs that should be covered via the general waste levy. Costs should be borne by those who incur them. The municipality of Peel and Maas has a well-functioning recycling center where residents can dispose of bulky waste at low cost.

121901.explanation: Accessibility is important. The municipality takes responsibility for public buildings. Financial support for entrepreneurs in this area goes too far. The municipality can stimulate, think along with entrepreneurs, and ensure that national laws and regulations are applied.

121903.explanation: Under certain conditions, supporting people on social assistance benefits (such as when children are involved) is necessary. Everyone should be able to participate. Of course, this must be realistic. This calls for tailored solutions from the municipality.

121905.explanation: Music lessons are very important for the development of our children and fall under culture and education. Music lessons, especially instruments, are not cheap. Additional support in this area is therefore important to CDA Peel and Maas.

121906.explanation: (Sports) associations deserve support. This does not necessarily have to be financial. Sometimes the municipality can step in in exceptional situations. It is up to the (sports) association itself to maintain its viability.

121910.explanation: We are not in favor of standard camera surveillance. In some situations, such as after certain incidents, temporary camera surveillance can be a solution.

121913.explanation: We want our own residents to have enough opportunities to continue living in the municipality. At the same time, the principles that everyone has the right to freely choose where to live and to fair allocation are important. In some situations, deviations can be made. For example: to stimulate housing turnover.

121915.explanation: This is an undesirable development; parks are often not designed for this in terms of infrastructure (safety). Holiday homes are not a structural solution to the housing shortage. By allocating residential zones, this can become a responsibility for the municipality and result in community expenses.

121916.explanation: We cannot meet the large housing demand with infill alone. The CDA supports housing in the core-edge zone because it contributes to the availability of locations and is a natural development. The priority for the outer areas is the agricultural sector. Adding a small street on the edge is fine.

121917.explanation: Housing status holders is distributed nationally. Each municipality receives a task. We will address our housing assignment for status holders in consultation with housing corporations. The regional housing regulation also provides priority for our own residents.

121918.explanation: We believe that the most vulnerable residents deserve this protection.

121919.explanation: We will always support vulnerable residents and at least follow national policy. The focus is on how these residents can participate in society. Everyone deserves the chance to proudly earn their own income or actively contribute to our community.

121922.explanation: Safety comes first. In areas where 30 km/h is appropriate, that is fine. This requires customization. Roads within built-up areas have specific classifications (such as residential street, through route, access road).

121923.explanation: We gladly support freedom of choice in healthcare. This does require realism. Care providers are independent (commercial) enterprises and decide where they are active. The municipality's influence is limited. Religion or belief plays no role in tender processes.

121925.explanation: Peel and Maas’s financial position is sound due to prudent financial policy. There is room for a limited increase in local taxes by applying the current annual inflation correction. Municipal taxes are low compared to other municipalities. Stable policy.

121927.explanation: The current rate is not too high and is sufficient. Tourists also use our facilities. Compared to other municipalities, we do not charge excessively high rates.

121928.explanation: We are in favor of expanding business parks, especially for our local entrepreneurs. Facilitating this means that businesses and thus employment are retained for the municipality. This is important both economically and for village livability and socially.

121930.explanation: Housing status holders is regulated nationally. We will address our annual assignment in consultation with housing corporations. We are satisfied with the regional housing regulation that manages this, as we also want our own residents (statement 12) to receive priority.

121932.explanation: Agricultural land is meant for food production. We believe solar panels belong on the roofs of houses and (business) buildings. Besides solar panels, there are more opportunities for alternative energy sources, and we gladly encourage those.

121934.explanation: In a water-rich country like the Netherlands, we believe it is important that children learn to swim early. Parents have their own responsibility in this. In special situations, they can request financial support from the municipality.

121935.explanation: We are not in favor of coffeeshops in our municipality. Establishing a coffeeshop increases the chance of additional nuisance.

121936.explanation: The CDA chooses to add homes with attention to youth and seniors and to promote housing turnover. At the same time, we must consider the local situation of supply and demand and spatial possibilities. Being rigid with this rule can delay housing construction.

121941.explanation: In some places, intensive livestock farming stops, and in others, it develops further. Where possible, this is acceptable. Likewise, entrepreneurs choosing extensive/nature-inclusive farming where feasible is fine. Food security, food safety, and affordable food are important.

121942.explanation: As CDA Peel and Maas, we stand for good information provision to all age groups, especially our elderly who often prefer a physical newspaper delivered weekly to their homes. We, as a municipality, have a responsibility here.

121887.explanation: Every four years there are elections where everyone can express their opinion. Additionally, every resident can approach the council members. We are, of course, open to this.

121890.explanation: We want to actively contribute to the expansion of agricultural businesses. If there are no restrictions from the environment, we can generally allow this.

121893.explanation: As soon as the spread law is repealed and pressure on asylum inflow decreases, we want to close emergency shelters as soon as possible.

121898.explanation: If residents want to organize a fireworks show, the municipality can assist with permits or other matters. Organizing a fireworks show is not a task of the municipality itself.

121899.explanation: All waste is collected in Peel and Maas within existing budgets and taxes. No extra costs will be added.

121901.explanation: Accessibility is important, but entrepreneurs are primarily responsible themselves. The municipality can encourage or cooperate where necessary but will not routinely provide financial support.

121903.explanation: Participating in sports is important, but support should be targeted and customized, for example through existing schemes like the Leergeld Foundation and the youth, sports, and culture fund, not by automatic payment of memberships.

121905.explanation: Music education is valuable, but the municipality should primarily stimulate this through schools and existing programs.

121906.explanation: We want to treat every sports club in Peel and Maas equally. If financial problems arise due to certain circumstances, we certainly want to help. If the club becomes unviable, for example due to too few members, we will not fund keeping the club afloat.

121910.explanation: In principle, we are cautious about camera surveillance because it infringes on everyone's privacy. When the safety situation demands, camera surveillance can be used where necessary.

121913.explanation: We want to give as much priority as possible to our own residents, especially during housing shortages. Rules will be established for allocating social rental homes to our own residents.

121915.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation. Permanent residence should not become a general rule.

121916.explanation: Only with large housing development plans can we truly address the housing shortage. We want to realize a new residential district near every village.

121917.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits may be expected to perform a suitable, socially meaningful counter-performance, taking their abilities into account.

121918.explanation: We are not in favor of permanently forgiving debts. We help everyone who needs it with debt counseling and special assistance. Early detection is important.

121919.explanation: In Peel and Maas, we follow national policy of 120% of the social minimum. This is national policy, and we intend to continue following it.

121922.explanation: Customization is better than a general 30 km/h rule. Where possible, 50 km/h remains allowed.

121923.explanation: In times of scarcity in healthcare, we are glad everyone can receive the care they need.

121925.explanation: In recent years, Peel and Maas had surplus funds. It is therefore fair that this money is returned to residents through lower taxes.

121927.explanation: If the costs for tourists are lower than the revenues, we want to reduce the tourist tax.

121928.explanation: We want to provide space for companies that want to grow. We also want to attract high-quality knowledge companies. To this end, we want to offer space by expanding business parks.

121930.explanation: Everyone deserves equal treatment in Peel and Maas, so no priority for status holders.

121932.explanation: Agricultural land is primarily for farmers. Only with smart combinations with other energy sources can agricultural land possibly be used for solar panels.

121934.explanation: School swimming would fit well with education, but this should be arranged by the national government.

121935.explanation: We are against the establishment of a coffee shop. The risk of nuisance is too great for us.

121936.explanation: For us, the most important thing is that plans proceed. If a plan is not feasible due to the 30% rule, then we prefer fewer social rental homes.

121941.explanation: New developments of livestock farms are undesirable to us. Existing permits and rights are respected, including for barns that are not yet built, as long as their permits have not been withdrawn.

121942.explanation: Since the disappearance of Hallo Peel and Maas, a large gap has opened in providing information to residents, while we consider this very important.

121887.explanation: In our municipality, we work with self-management and participation, and in some areas the government takes the lead. A referendum is generally simplistic and does not allow for dialogue between residents.

121890.explanation: Not always and not everywhere, but only if more inclusive agriculture becomes possible alongside it.

121893.explanation: Sheltering asylum seekers is our moral duty.

121898.explanation: Depends on the need: it is still unclear how large that is.

121899.explanation: The recycling center currently meets the needs, including for bulky waste.

121901.explanation: That is primarily a matter for the entrepreneurs themselves; only where it is impossible for them can the municipality intervene.

121903.explanation: Where necessary. Everyone must be able to participate.

121905.explanation: Music lessons are not a luxury but very important for the development of children and culture in general. The effect of this course must be well monitored.

121906.explanation: Not necessarily; however, the municipality can bring parties together to collaborate.

121910.explanation: Camera surveillance does not add much at the moment and hardly contributes to the feeling of safety.

121913.explanation: There is a need for social rental homes among current residents, but also from people coming from outside. They also have the right to settle here, so there should be a balance.

121915.explanation: Living in holiday parks is not a good solution for the housing shortage, due to the facilities but also due to failed supervision. The solution is more building in the neighborhoods.

121916.explanation: Building outside the cores causes urban sprawl in the rural area. However, adding a 'small street' at the edges of the core zones should be allowed.

121917.explanation: The group receiving social assistance benefits is very diverse; you cannot just implement one measure. We look at what someone can and wants, under the motto: 'everyone should be able to participate.'

121918.explanation: In our municipality, we take into account people with low incomes. Waiving municipal taxes is part of that.

121919.explanation: Yes, people with a very low income but just above the minimum income are excluded everywhere, and that group is often worse off than people with social assistance benefits.

121922.explanation: Yes, within the built-up area the street should again belong to children, cyclists, and pedestrians. This way we make public space in the cores places of meeting again, instead of public spaces dominated by cars.

121923.explanation: Where possible, but healthcare is already suffering from staff shortages, so that will not always be possible.

121925.explanation: No. If everyone contributes, we can collectively achieve more.

121927.explanation: No, tourists also use our facilities.

121928.explanation: Two business parks will expand in the near future. Rather than small parcels of business parks in every village, it would be better to create one large business park in the municipality.

121930.explanation: Refugees with status, like many other groups, face disadvantages in the housing market and should therefore be given some priority—just like divorced people with children, etc.

121932.explanation: In places where this can be well integrated and does not infringe on the landscape quality, solar parks can be established.

121934.explanation: Swimming is a necessary skill in this water-rich country. For many families, it is too expensive. We also provide traffic lessons at school.

121935.explanation: If there is demonstrable need, a coffee shop can be permitted.

121936.explanation: Wholeheartedly agree. To make this possible, we will have to build even more than 30% in the near future as a catch-up effort.

121941.explanation: Agree. Because of nitrogen, fine dust, odor, the appearance of the countryside, and animal suffering, this is an outdated way to conduct livestock farming.

121942.explanation: The municipality can provide space to bundle the existing digital village magazines on its website, so they become easily accessible to everyone.

121887.explanation: D66 wants to actively involve residents in decision-making through, for example, citizens' motions and accessible agenda proposals. Residents can put topics on the agenda themselves and propose local initiatives. A referendum is one of the options.

121890.explanation: D66 chooses customization. Not everything can be done everywhere. We give farmers space to undertake, as long as initiatives fit the landscape, environment, and a sustainable future for the farm.

121893.explanation: Peel and Maas takes responsibility for humane reception and good integration.

121898.explanation: D66 is positive about a municipal fireworks show as a safe and joint alternative to consumer fireworks. This way, everyone can celebrate New Year’s Eve together with less nuisance, injury, and environmental damage.

121899.explanation: In Peel and Maas, bulky waste can be collected at home; residents pay a contribution to fairly share collective costs and responsible waste processing. D66 wants waste policy that stimulates less waste.

121901.explanation: Accessibility is a basic condition for an inclusive society. D66 does not want to financially obligate entrepreneurs but does want to encourage and support them to make their buildings more accessible so that everyone can participate.

121903.explanation: Poverty should never limit participation. Those with little must still be able to play sports, make music, or join in. For that, minimum support arrangements are important.

121905.explanation: Culture and talent development are part of a rich school day. D66 wants every child to come into contact with music, where the form and financing—subsidy or other support—are defined together with schools and cultural partners.

121906.explanation: Associations are the heart of the community and deserve support, even when times are tough.

121910.explanation: Camera surveillance can be useful in some situations, but must not come at the expense of privacy.

121913.explanation: D66 chooses a fair and transparent allocation policy without exclusion. Not only current residents, but also people who want to live in Peel and Maas or who have previously lived here and want to return, should have equal opportunities for social rental housing.

121915.explanation: Holiday homes are not a structural solution for the housing shortage.

121916.explanation: Peel and Maas faces a major housing challenge. Building only within existing village boundaries is insufficient to meet the demand. D66 therefore opts for a combination of infill and expansion, so that enough affordable homes become available.

121917.explanation: Participation is important, but always based on tailored approaches and voluntariness.

121918.explanation: D66 wants to combat poverty, not worsen it. We choose a people-centered approach to debt, based on trust, personal strength, and future perspective.

121919.explanation: More people need help due to rising costs.

121922.explanation: Lowering the maximum speed to 30 km/h within built-up areas significantly improves traffic safety, especially for vulnerable road users such as children and the elderly.

121923.explanation: D66 places choice freedom and appropriate care at the center.

121925.explanation: Good governance requires clear financial choices. D66 wants to continue investing in the future of Peel and Maas in housing, a healthy generation, and a strong economy. At the same time, the basics remain in order. Property tax for residents will not rise faster than inflation. In case of deficits, we make explicit choices about what cannot be done, and ensure that plans are always covered. We do not pass bills on to the future and keep Peel and Maas’s financial position solid.

121927.explanation: There is a tourist tax in Peel and Maas. D66 finds it important that both tourists and labor migrants who use municipal facilities pay a fair contribution.

121928.explanation: D66 considers it important that there is room for sustainable economic growth and local employment. Expanding business parks can contribute to a strong economy, provided it happens carefully with consideration for sustainability, accessibility, and good integration into the surroundings.

121930.explanation: The municipality has a legal obligation to house refugees with residence permits. Additionally, there are several other target groups that have priority in the allocation of social rental housing, such as urgent seekers and people exiting mental health care.

121932.explanation: D66 prefers solar panels on roofs. At the same time, we see that local energy generation on farmland can offer farmers a new income model. Therefore, D66 chooses tailored solutions, seeking a good balance between energy production, landscape, and agriculture.

121934.explanation: D66 aims for the healthiest generation ever but does not want to impose anything on schools. We will investigate whether, in the long term, swimming can become part of the broad daily offer.

121935.explanation: D66 supports regulation and safety, with local consideration.

121936.explanation: D66 finds it important that enough social rental homes are built but prefers flexibility per project instead of a fixed percentage. For each location and project, feasibility and appropriateness are assessed to create a balanced mix of homes for different target groups without unnecessarily limiting building plans.

121941.explanation: D66 opposes further growth of intensive livestock farming in Peel and Maas. Expanding pig farms causes extra environmental burden and contradicts our commitment to a healthy living environment for residents. We therefore choose sustainable agriculture, encouraging farmers to invest in innovative and environmentally friendly alternatives.

121942.explanation: Independent journalism is important but not through a municipal magazine.

121887.explanation: The BBB believes that residents should have a voice in important developments in their own area.

121890.explanation: The BBB supports the establishment of more farm shops. The food is fresh, meets all quality requirements, and the farmer receives a fair price.

121893.explanation: The BBB believes the municipality has provided sufficient shelter in recent years. We think neighboring municipalities should now take their turn. The vacated housing can be used for everyone urgently looking for a home.

121898.explanation: The BBB believes that setting off fireworks is not the responsibility of the municipality.

121899.explanation: The BBB believes that waste costs should remain low so that waste taxes can be reduced. The BBB believes the municipality must enforce more actively against illegal dumping of waste.

121901.explanation: The BBB finds accessibility important for all residents. For public buildings, the municipality is responsible; for businesses, this is the entrepreneur’s responsibility.

121903.explanation: The BBB does not take this for granted.

121905.explanation: The BBB finds it important that music lessons at school are accessible to all children.

121906.explanation: The BBB believes associations are primarily responsible for their own finances. The municipality can provide one-time financial assistance.

121910.explanation: The BBB believes that temporary camera surveillance helps to combat nuisance and crime.

121913.explanation: The BBB believes that priority for status holders in the allocation of social rental homes should end.

121915.explanation: The BBB believes people urgently looking for housing should have the option to live in a holiday home. Allowing occupancy of holiday homes reduces pressure on the housing market.

121916.explanation: The BBB believes the municipality of Peel and Maas is too strict regarding building in rural areas. However, we believe that building in rural areas should not restrict businesses located there.

121917.explanation: The BBB believes that people on welfare benefits should actively contribute to the community.

121918.explanation: The BBB believes municipal taxes for low-income people should be able to be waived.

121919.explanation: The BBB believes tailored approaches must be possible. BBB Peel and Maas does not want to change the income limit previously set by the municipal council.

121922.explanation: The BBB believes tailored approaches must be applied. 50 km/h where possible, 30 km/h where the situation requires it.

121923.explanation: The BBB believes that availability of care should never depend on religion, culture, or belief.

121925.explanation: In recent years, the municipality had millions left over yet still raised the property tax (ozb) automatically every year. BBB was the only party to vote against this. We believe residents should not be milked by the municipality.

121927.explanation: BBB believes that the tourist tax should be indexed annually.

121928.explanation: BBB thinks that where there is demand, business parks may be expanded for local companies. However, this should not impede other businesses in the rural area.

121930.explanation: BBB believes there should be an end to the priority scheme for status holders. Status holders are neither more nor less than other residents in the municipality seeking social rental housing.

121932.explanation: BBB believes that agricultural land and horticultural greenhouses should be used for food production. We believe solar panels belong on roofs.

121934.explanation: BBB considers being able to swim in a country like the Netherlands essential. Additionally, extra physical activity is good for children.

121935.explanation: BBB believes that coffeeshops do not belong in Peel and Maas.

121936.explanation: BBB believes housing must be built for all target groups, including social rental homes.

121941.explanation: BBB opposes a ban on expansions. Farmers ensure food safety and security. Where possible, businesses should have the option to expand.

121942.explanation: BBB regrets that the municipality decided to end collaboration with Hallo. A free door-to-door paper ensures that you also know what is happening in other villages.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate funds for a new free door-to-door newspaper in Peel en Maas.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The residents of Peel en Maas should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Peel en Maas moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Peel en Maas should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.text: In Peel en Maas moet meer

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Peel en Maas should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Peel en Maas moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Business parks in Peel en Maas may be expanded.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Peel en Maas should ban the expansion of existing pig farms.
